mission.title: opentelemetry-collector: [Documentation] Explain the exporter queue failure scenario
mission.description: [Documentation] Explain the exporter queue failure scenario. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentelemetry-collector deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentelemetry-collector version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
helm list -n opentelemetry-collector 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentelemetry-collector installation.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-collector configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-collector configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-collector
```
No this is specific to documentation.

Add a paragraph in [exporter helper](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/tree/main/exporter/exporterhelper) explaining the failure scenarios:
- if new items can't be added to the queue for

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Documentation] Explain the exporter queue failure scenario
steps.description:
Adds documentation clarifying that data is dropped when it cannot be enqueued
(e.g., queue at capacity or persistent storage cannot accept writes).

This happens before exporter retry logic.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-collector --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Documentation] Explain the exporter queue failure scenario" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds documentation clarifying that data is dropped when it cannot be enqueued
(e.g., queue at capacity or persistent storage cannot accept writes).

This happens before exporter retry logic.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-collector installed or the issue environment reproducible.
